title: Plurals
intro: Making plurals in Spanish is straightforward — just follow three simple rules based on what letter the word ends in.

sections.title: The three plural rules
rows: a vowel (a, e, i, o, u)

sections.title: Days of the week — no change!
sections.content:
Days ending in -s are the same in singular and plural:
• el lunes → los lunes (Monday / Mondays)
• el viernes → los viernes (Friday / Fridays)
Only the article changes: el → los.

sections.title: Accent marks may shift
sections.content:
Sometimes adding -es removes or adds an accent:
• el joven → los jóvenes (the young person / people)
• el examen → los exámenes (the exam / exams)
Don't worry — you'll pick these up naturally with practice.

items.es: Los libros están en la mesa.
items.en: The books are on the table.

questions.explanation: libro ends in a vowel (-o) → add -s → libros ✓
ciudad ends in a consonant (-d) → add -es → ciudades ✓
lápiz ends in -z → change z to c, add -es → lápices ✓

questions.prompt: What is the plural of 'el lunes' (Monday)?
questions.explanation: Days ending in -s don't change! Only the article changes: el lunes → los lunes ✓

questions.prompt: What is the plural of 'la flor' (the flower)?
questions.explanation: flor ends in a consonant (-r) → add -es → flores ✓
lápiz → lápices (z changes to c before -es) ✓

questions.prompt: What is the plural of 'el hotel'?
questions.explanation: hotel ends in consonant (-l) → add -es → hoteles ✓
